questions.question: What is the primary function of the Genero Ghost Client (GGC)?

options.label: A
options.text: To provide a graphical interface for administrative database management.
options.rationale: Database management is handled via native drivers and specialized tools, not the Ghost Client.

options.label: B
options.text: To enable headless regression and load testing by simulating multiple users.
options.rationale: The Ghost Client is designed to hit the system with simulated user traffic to validate stability and performance before release.

options.label: C
options.text: To act as a secure proxy for mobile device connections in the cloud.
options.rationale: Cloud connectivity and mobile access are handled by the Genero Application Server and specific mobile clients.

options.label: D
options.text: To automatically convert terminal-based forms into HTML5 web pages.
options.rationale: This transformation is the responsibility of the Genero Browser Client, not the testing client.

questions.question: Which Genero Enterprise component is responsible for transforming applications into a zero-footprint HTML5/CSS3 web solution?

options.label: A
options.rationale: The GDC is intended for native desktop deployment rather than zero-footprint browser access.

options.label: B
options.rationale: BDL is the programming language itself, which requires a specific client to render the interface.

options.label: C
options.rationale: The GBC allows applications to run in standard browsers like Chrome or Edge without any local installation or plugins.

options.label: D
options.rationale: This tool is focused on the design and generation of high-volume reports rather than the application UI.

questions.question: How does the Genero Application Server (GAS) ensure fault isolation in mission-critical environments?

options.label: A
options.text: By running each user session in a separate, isolated process.
options.rationale: This architecture ensures that a critical failure in one session does not impact other connected users.

options.label: B
options.text: By automatically restarting the entire server whenever an error occurs.
options.rationale: Restarting the whole server would disrupt all users, which contradicts the goal of business continuity.

options.label: C
options.text: By encrypting all business logic before it is sent to the client device.
options.rationale: While security is important, encryption does not provide session-level fault isolation or process stability.

options.label: D
options.text: By utilizing a monolithic thread-based model to manage all active users.
options.rationale: Monolithic models are risky because a single thread crash can crash the entire application for all users.

questions.question: What is the core high-level programming language used within the Genero Enterprise platform?

options.label: A
options.rationale: While Genero integrates with Java, it uses its own specialized language for business logic development.

options.label: B
options.rationale: BDL is the readable 4GL syntax that compiles to p-code and serves as the successor to Informix 4GL.

options.label: C
options.rationale: Python is not the primary language of the Genero platform, although external scripts might be used in DevOps.

options.label: D
options.rationale: Genero compiles to p-code for a virtual machine rather than generating C or C++ code directly.

questions.question: Which tool provides developers with a visual productivity environment for drag-and-drop form layout and graphical workflow modeling?

options.label: A
options.rationale: VS Code is supported for coding and debugging, but it is not the primary tool for visual form and report design.

options.label: B
options.rationale: Genero Studio is the specialized IDE designed for visual interface design, project management, and report modeling.

options.label: C
options.rationale: The GAS is a runtime component used for hosting applications, not a design-time development tool.

options.label: D
options.rationale: DB Sync is a utility for database management and synchronization, not for UI design.

questions.question: What is a major advantage of using Genero Mobile (GMA/GMI) for field operations over a standard web application?

options.label: A
options.text: It requires a constant internet connection to function.
options.rationale: Genero Mobile is specifically noted for its offline-first capabilities, allowing logic to run without connectivity.

options.label: B
options.text: It runs inside a standard mobile browser like Safari.
options.rationale: While web clients exist, GMA and GMI are true native apps that run directly on the operating system.

options.label: C
options.text: It provides native access to hardware like GPS, cameras, and scanners.
options.rationale: Native mobile apps can directly integrate with device hardware, which is critical for mission-critical field work.

options.label: D
options.text: It prevents developers from using the same codebase for desktop versions.
options.rationale: A key benefit of Genero is the 'Develop Once, Deploy Anywhere' philosophy using a single codebase.

questions.question: The Genero architecture compiles BDL code into portable p-code. Which component executes this p-code to ensure OS-agnostic behavior?

options.label: A
options.rationale: Genero uses its own stable virtual machine rather than the Java Virtual Machine.

options.label: B
options.rationale: The Genero VM runs the p-code and maintains consistency across Linux, Windows, and Unix platforms.

options.label: C
options.rationale: While the Browser Client uses JS for rendering, the core business logic is executed by the backend VM.

options.label: D
options.rationale: Genero does not generate C code like legacy Informix 4GL; it relies on a virtual machine for execution.

questions.question: Which Genero tool is used to containerize backend components like GAS and FGL for deployment in Kubernetes clusters?

options.label: A
options.rationale: GCI provides the tools and images needed to integrate Genero applications into Docker and Kubernetes environments.

options.label: B
options.rationale: GGC is used for testing and simulation, not for container orchestration or packaging.

options.label: C
options.rationale: GDC is a frontend client for desktop users and is not involved in backend containerization.

options.label: D
options.rationale: The GRE is the runtime engine for processing reports, not a container management tool.

questions.question: In the context of the Genero Report Writer, what is the role of the Genero Report Engine (GRE)?

options.label: A
options.text: It is a visual tool used to drag and drop elements onto a report layout.
options.rationale: The visual design is handled by the Report Designer/Studio, while the GRE is the runtime processor.

options.label: B
options.text: It acts as a high-performance engine for processing and rendering reports from XML data streams.
options.rationale: The GRE is the core runtime component that takes data and templates to produce finalized outputs like PDFs.

options.label: C
options.text: It is a specialized database used only for storing report history.
options.rationale: Genero is database-independent and the GRE processes live data streams rather than acting as storage.

options.label: D
options.text: It serves as the identity provider for secure report access.
options.rationale: Security and authentication are handled by the SSO/IDP components, not the report engine.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of the Genero Chatbot introduced in Genero Enterprise v6?

options.label: A
options.text: It automatically rewrites legacy Java code into BDL syntax.
options.rationale: The chatbot is for developer support and documentation search, not automated code translation from other languages.

options.label: B
options.text: It provides instant answers and code examples grounded in official documentation using conversational AI.
options.rationale: The chatbot uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to help developers resolve issues and find documentation faster.

options.label: C
options.text: It acts as a replacement for the Genero Studio debugging tools.
options.rationale: While helpful, it is a support tool and does not replace the functional IDE debugging capabilities.

options.label: D
options.text: It manages the synchronization between local Git repositories and cloud servers.
options.rationale: Git integration is a feature of the development tooling, not a conversational AI chatbot.

questions.question: Which component allows for a native desktop deployment that can access local hardware and operate with high-performance UI on Windows, Linux, or macOS?

options.label: A
options.rationale: The Browser Client is constrained by the browser sandbox and is not a native desktop application.

options.label: B
options.rationale: The GDC provides the most powerful desktop experience with native performance and platform-specific feature access.

options.label: C
options.rationale: The GAS is the backend server component, not the client-side UI renderer for desktop users.

options.label: D
options.rationale: GMA is specifically for Android mobile devices, not desktop operating systems like Windows or macOS.

questions.question: Genero Report Writer supports the generation of $Factur-X$ compliant invoices. What is $Factur-X$?

options.label: A
options.text: A specialized database driver for high-speed report queries.
options.rationale: Factur-X is a document standard, not a technical database driver.

options.label: B
options.text: An automated testing script used for validating UI responsiveness.
options.rationale: UI responsiveness is tested via the Ghost Client, not through invoice standards.

options.label: C
options.text: A global standard for electronic invoicing that combines PDF data with XML metadata.
options.rationale: Factur-X is the standard for e-invoicing that Genero supports to ensure regulatory compliance.

options.label: D
options.text: A proprietary compression algorithm used to reduce the size of report files.
options.rationale: Genero uses standard technologies; Factur-X is a compliance standard rather than a compression method.

questions.question: Which monitoring tool integration allows IT staff to track Genero Application Server (GAS) health and trigger alerts for infrastructure monitoring?

options.label: A
options.rationale: Genero Enterprise v6 explicitly includes Prometheus/Grafana integration for real-time application and server performance tracking.

options.label: B
options.rationale: Spark is for big data processing and is not the primary monitoring tool integrated into the GAS.

options.label: C
options.rationale: Google Analytics is for web traffic analysis, not for deep infrastructure monitoring of application server health.

options.label: D
options.rationale: While Excel can be a report target, it is not used for real-time infrastructure health monitoring and alerts.

questions.question: What distinguishes Genero’s 'Stateful Application Architecture' from a standard stateless web architecture?

options.label: A
options.text: It requires the client to store all business logic and validation rules locally.
options.rationale: Genero's logic runs securely on the server; the stateful nature refers to the persistent backend process.

options.label: B
options.text: It assigns a persistent backend process (VM) to each user session to preserve business context on the server.
options.rationale: This simplifies complex, multi-step workflows by keeping data and context active on the server throughout the session.

options.label: C
options.text: It prevents the application from being deployed in a cloud environment.
options.rationale: Genero is cloud-ready and supports session management and load balancing within cloud infrastructures.

options.label: D
options.text: It forces the user to log in again every time they navigate to a new screen.
options.rationale: A stateful architecture actually prevents this by maintaining the user's session context automatically.

questions.question: Which component enables Genero developers to expose their business logic as RESTful web services for third-party integration?

options.label: A
options.rationale: GST is the design environment where the services are defined, but the runtime component is required for communication.

options.label: B
options.text: Genero Web Services (GWS) and BDL libraries
options.rationale: GWS and the built-in BDL libraries (JSON, HTTP, XML) provide the native capability to communicate with external web apps and APIs.

options.label: C
options.rationale: GGC tests existing interfaces but does not serve as the production gateway for RESTful API communication.

options.label: D
options.rationale: The GRE is dedicated to document output rather than real-time web service logic execution.
